steps.title: Isolate the absolute value
steps.content: Divide both sides of $$2|x-5| = k$$ by 2: $$|x-5| = \frac{k}{2}$$

steps.title: Determine when the absolute value equation has exactly one solution
steps.content:
For any constant c, the equation |x - 5| = c has:
  • No solution when c < 0 (absolute value can’t be negative)
  • Exactly one solution when c = 0 (only x = 5 works)
  • Exactly two solutions when c > 0 (one on each side of 5)
So exactly one solution requires k/2 = 0.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — '−5 only': k/2 = −5 means |x−5| = −5, which has no solutions at all. ✗
  • Choice B — '−5 or 5': k/2 = −5 gives no solutions; k/2 = 5 gives two solutions. Neither yields exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice C — '0 only': k/2 = 0 means |x−5| = 0, so x = 5 is the unique solution. ✓
  • Choice D — '5 only': k/2 = 5 means |x−5| = 5, giving x = 10 and x = 0 — two solutions, not one. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
